words.definitionEn: a complete lack of order
words.exampleEn: economic/political/domestic chaos

words.definitionEn: to be typical of a person, place or thing
words.exampleEn: the rolling hills that characterize this part of England

words.definitionEn: the power of pleasing or attracting people
words.exampleEn: He was a man of great charm.

words.definitionEn: a written statement describing the rights that a particular group of people should have
words.exampleEn: the European Social Charter of workers’ rights

words.definitionEn: lasting for a long time; difficult to cure
words.exampleEn: chronic bronchitis/arthritis/asthma

words.definitionEn: a thick, solid piece that has been cut or broken off something
words.exampleEn: a chunk of cheese/masonry

words.definitionEn: when a liquid, gas or air circulates or is circulated, it moves continuously around a place or system
words.exampleEn: The condition prevents the blood from circulating freely.

words.definitionEn: the movement of blood around the body
words.exampleEn: Regular exercise will improve blood circulation.

words.definitionEn: the legal right to belong to a particular country
words.exampleEn: They were granted full French citizenship.

words.definitionEn: officially connected with a town or city
words.exampleEn: civic buildings/leaders

words.definitionEn: connected with people who are not members of the armed forces or the police
words.exampleEn: He left the army and returned to civilian life.

words.definitionEn: a person who is not a member of the armed forces or the police
words.exampleEn: Two soldiers and one civilian were killed in the explosion.

words.definitionEn: the quality of being expressed clearly
words.exampleEn: a lack of clarity in the law

words.definitionEn: a short fight between two groups of people
words.exampleEn: Clashes broke out between police and demonstrators.

words.definitionEn: the act or process of putting people or things into a group or class (= of classifying them)
words.exampleEn: a style of music that defies classification (= is like no other)

words.definitionEn: to hold on tightly to somebody/something
words.exampleEn: cling to somebody/something, survivors clinging to a raft

words.definitionEn: to stick to something
words.exampleEn: Once in the bloodstream, the bacteria adhere to the surface of the red cells.

words.definitionEn: relating to the examination and treatment of patients and their illnesses
words.exampleEn: clinical research (= done on patients, not just considering theory)

words.definitionEn: next to something
words.exampleEn: The planes landed on adjacent runways.

words.definitionEn: the situation when a factory, school, hospital, etc. shuts permanently
words.exampleEn: factory closures

words.definitionEn: a group of things of the same type that grow or appear close together
words.exampleEn: The telescope is focused on a dense cluster of stars at the edge of the galaxy.

words.definitionEn: a government formed by two or more political parties working together
words.exampleEn: The two parties have formed a coalition.

words.definitionEn: of or near a coast
words.exampleEn: coastal waters/resorts/scenery

words.definitionEn: a drink usually made from a mixture of one or more spirits (= strong alcoholic drinks) and fruit juice. It can also be made without alcohol.
words.exampleEn: a cocktail bar/cabinet/lounge/shaker

words.definitionEn: connected with mental processes of understanding
words.exampleEn: a child’s cognitive development

words.definitionEn: a small change made to something in order to correct or improve it
words.exampleEn: I've made a few adjustments to the design.

words.definitionEn: to take place at the same time
words.exampleEn: It's a pity our trips to New York don't coincide.

words.definitionEn: to work together with somebody in order to produce or achieve something
words.exampleEn: Researchers around the world are collaborating to develop a new vaccine.

words.definitionEn: the act of working with another person or group of people to create or produce something
words.exampleEn: It was a collaboration that produced extremely useful results.

words.definitionEn: to manage and organize the affairs of a company, an organization, a country, etc.
words.exampleEn: to administer a charity/fund/school

words.definitionEn: done or shared by all members of a group of people; involving a whole group or society
words.exampleEn: collective leadership/decision-making/responsibility

words.definitionEn: an accident in which two vehicles or people crash into each other
words.exampleEn: collision between A and B, a collision between two trains

words.definitionEn: connected with or belonging to a country that controls another country
words.exampleEn: a colonial power

words.definitionEn: a journalist who writes regular articles for a newspaper or magazine
words.exampleEn: a newspaper columnist

words.definitionEn: fighting or a fight, especially during a time of war
words.exampleEn: in combat, He was killed in combat.

words.definitionEn: to stop something unpleasant or harmful from happening or from getting worse
words.exampleEn: measures to combat crime/inflation/unemployment/disease

words.definitionEn: connected with organizing the work of a business or an institution
words.exampleEn: an administrative job/assistant/error

words.definitionEn: to begin to happen; to begin something
words.exampleEn: The meeting is scheduled to commence at noon.

words.definitionEn: a spoken description of an event that is given while it is happening, especially on the radio or television
words.exampleEn: a sports commentary

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is to manage and organize the public or business affairs of a company or an institution, or a person who works in an office dealing with records, accounts, etc.
words.exampleEn: Such organizational decisions are made by the hospital administrators.

words.definitionEn: a person who describes an event while it is happening, especially on television or radio
words.exampleEn: a television/sports commentator

words.definitionEn: trade, especially between countries; the buying and selling of goods and services
words.exampleEn: Leaders of industry and commerce met at the summit in Paris.

words.definitionEn: a member of a commission (= an official group of people who are responsible for controlling something or finding out about something)
words.exampleEn: the Church Commissioners (= the group of people responsible for controlling the financial affairs of the Church of England)

words.definitionEn: a product or a raw material that can be bought and sold
words.exampleEn: rice, flour and other basic commodities

words.definitionEn: connected with communism
words.exampleEn: communist ideology

words.definitionEn: a person or an animal that travels with you or spends a lot of time with you
words.exampleEn: travelling companions

words.definitionEn: similar to somebody/something else and able to be compared
words.exampleEn: A comparable house in the south of the city would cost twice as much.

words.definitionEn: the act of accepting somebody into an institution, organization, etc.; the right to enter a place or to join an institution or organization
words.exampleEn: Hospital admission is not necessary in most cases.

words.definitionEn: a strong feeling of sympathy for people or animals who are suffering and a desire to help them
words.exampleEn: to feel/show compassion

words.definitionEn: to force somebody to do something; to make something necessary
words.exampleEn: compel somebody to do something, The law can compel fathers to make regular payments for their children.

words.definitionEn: that makes you pay attention to it because it is so interesting and exciting
words.exampleEn: Her latest book makes compelling reading.

words.definitionEn: to provide something good to balance or reduce the bad effects of damage, loss, etc.
words.exampleEn: Nothing can compensate for the loss of a loved one.

words.definitionEn: something, especially money, that somebody gives you because they have hurt you, or damaged something that you own; the act of giving this to somebody
words.exampleEn: to claim/award/receive compensation

words.definitionEn: the ability to do something well
words.exampleEn: to gain a high level of competence in English
